commands.review_summary:
The PR changes Claude OAuth `extra_usage` cost normalization to always divide `used_credits` and `monthly_limit` by 100 before building `ProviderCostSnapshot`, and updates Claude OAuth spend-limit tests.

Reproducibility: yes. source inspection gives a high-confidence path: on current main, a spend-limit-only OAuth payload with `monthly_limit=2000` and `used_credits=763` passes through the spend-limit major-unit exception and displays 100x too high. I did not run tests because this review was required to keep the checkout read-only.
commands.review_followup: Needs maintainer review or contributor live proof for the Claude OAuth unit assumption; automation cannot produce the affected account evidence.

target.title: Claude: normalize OAuth extra usage spend limit from minor units
target.body:
Fixes #1111.

## Summary
- Normalize Claude OAuth `extra_usage.used_credits` and `monthly_limit` from minor units to major currency units before creating `ProviderCostSnapshot`.
- Prevents recurrence of the #227-style 100x extra usage display bug.
- Example fixed behavior: `used_credits=763`, `monthly_limit=2000`, `currency=EUR` now displays as `€7.63 / €20.00`, while utilization remains ~`38.15%`.
- Scope is intentionally limited to Claude OAuth extra usage normalization.

## Scope
- Claude OAuth only
- No Cursor changes
- No formatter/UI changes
- No `ProviderCostSnapshot` semantic changes
- No Claude Web changes
- No provider fetcher or network behavior changes beyond the normalization boundary

## Validation
- `swift test --filter ClaudeOAuth`
- `swift test --filter Cursor`
- `swift test --filter MenuCard`
- `make check`
- `git diff --check`

Note: `swift test --filter Claude` previously hit an unrelated flaky `ClaudeOAuthCredentialsStoreSecurityCLITests` failure outside the #1111 files.
target.author: yuxin-qiao
